text: We thought what was cool got really bad really fast. The drinks were room temperature. There was absolutely no food. People had been there the entire day, drunk, kind of crazy at this point, and starving. When did you realize there wasn't going to be a concert? We got to the actual festival location. It was ten o'clock, and right when we got there, we realized this was not going to be a real thing. We found out every band had canceled that night, and we also found out that they were not going to have enough tents to supply for the entire festival. So I know everyone keeps saying it online on the internet, and it's kind of crazy, but it was literally like the Hunger Games. People were sprinting, trying to find a place to sleep. Okay, kind of a high class Hunger Games, but I guess you should have followed your initial instinct, huh? Five hundred dollars is a little too good to be true. It was funny. About the day before the festival, my parents called me and they're like, "Hey, Brett, you know, we heard some pretty weird rumors about this festival. Are you sure you want to go?" And I was like, "Mom, come on, absolutely. I'm going to go to this thing. This is way too cool." But I guess she was right.
